principle: Position the single most important metric in the top-left card of every dashboard — eye tracking on information-dense screens shows users fixate top-left first and use it to calibrate the scale of all other numbers
explanation: X.ui|dashboard_primary_metric: random_metric_placement -> user_hunts_for_the_number_they_care_about; top_left_rule: most_business_critical_KPI(revenue+DAU+churn) always_top_left_card; supporting_metrics: clockwise_from_primary; secondary_row: actionable_metrics; card_structure: metric_value+trend_vs_prior_period+sparkline; color: green_delta_positive+red_delta_negative; NO:burying_primary_KPI_in_middle NO:all_metrics_same_visual_weight; eg: 6_equal_cards >> primary_KPI_top_left_2x_size+supporting_metrics_smaller

principle: Use sparklines for trend context inside metric cards and full charts only when the user needs to interact with the data — full charts in a summary dashboard consume space without adding actionable detail
explanation: X.ui|sparklines_vs_full_charts: full_chart_per_KPI_card -> dashboard_too_dense+overwhelming; sparkline: 40-60px_tall_line_chart_embedded_in_card -> shows_direction+volatility without_needing_axes_or_labels; use_full_chart: dedicated_analytics_page+when_user_needs_drill_down+hover_tooltips; spark_lines: always_without_axes_labels+just_the_shape; NO:mini_pie_charts_in_cards(unreadable_at_small_size) NO:full_bar_chart_in_every_summary_card; eg: full_30-day_chart_in_KPI_card >> sparkline_embedded_in_metric_card

principle: Select chart type by the relationship being shown: bar for comparison, line for trend over time, scatter for correlation, pie only for two-part wholes
explanation: X.ui|chart_type_selection: wrong_chart_type -> data_relationship_misrepresented; rules: bar: compare_discrete_categories(A_vs_B_vs_C); line: continuous_change_over_time(daily_revenue); scatter: relationship_between_two_variables; pie: ONLY_two_or_three_slices_that_sum_to_whole(market_share); heat_map: patterns_across_two_dimensions; NO:pie_chart_with_7+_slices(impossible_to_compare) NO:bar_chart_for_continuous_time_series; eg: 8-slice_pie_chart >> horizontal_bar_chart_with_sorted_values

principle: Use sequential color palettes for ordered data and qualitative palettes for categorical data — using a rainbow palette for ordered data makes the viewer perceive false categorical groupings
explanation: X.ui|data_viz_color: rainbow_palette_for_temperature_map -> no_perceptual_order; correct_palettes: sequential(single_hue_light-to-dark) for ordered_data(low_to_high); diverging(two_hues_meeting_at_neutral) for_data_with_meaningful_midpoint(positive_vs_negative); qualitative(distinct_hues) for_unordered_categories; max_qualitative_colors: 8(beyond_that_pairs_become_indistinguishable); colorblind_safe: avoid_red+green_only_encoding; NO:rainbow_for_continuous_data NO:red+green_encoding_without_shape_or_label; eg: rainbow_heatmap >> single_hue_sequential_palette

principle: Design first-run empty dashboard state as a guided setup flow not a blank screen — new users who see empty charts with no guidance assume the product is broken or they set it up wrong
explanation: X.ui|empty_dashboard: blank_charts_on_signup -> user_confused+churns_immediately; empty_dashboard_pattern: (1)onboarding_checklist_widget(connect_data+invite_team+configure) (2)sample_data_preview_with_"Using_sample_data"_badge (3)setup_CTA_per_widget; progress: show_%_setup_complete; trigger_real_data: checklist_item_checked -> replace_sample_with_real; NO:empty_charts_with_no_guidance NO:tooltip_only_onboarding_without_data_sample; eg: blank_dashboard_on_new_account >> sample_data+guided_setup_checklist

principle: Make every metric on a dashboard directly actionable — a metric without a path to improve it is surveillance, not a tool
explanation: X.ui|actionable_metrics: metric_displayed_without_action_path -> user_knows_number_but_cannot_respond; action_pattern: each_KPI_card_links_to_relevant_action_or_detail_view; examples: bounce_rate_card -> link_to_pages_report; churn_metric -> link_to_at_risk_users_list; revenue_metric -> link_to_invoices; CTA_on_card: subtle(icon_or_"View_details"); alarm_metrics: threshold_exceeded -> highlight_card+surface_recommended_action; NO:metrics_with_no_navigation_path NO:separate_"actions"_page_with_no_metric_connection; eg: churn_number_only >> churn_number+"View_at_risk_users"_link

principle: Transform wide data tables into card lists on mobile — tables wider than the viewport force horizontal scrolling which defeats the purpose of mobile dashboards
explanation: X.ux|responsive_tables: table_on_mobile: horizontal_scroll -> user_loses_row_context; card_list_transform: each_table_row_becomes_a_card(label:value_pairs_stacked); priority_columns: show_2-3_most_important_columns+"More"_expand; sticky_first_column: for_tables_that_must_stay_tabular; CSS: @media(max-width:768px){table{display:block} tr{display:card_layout}}; NO:horizontal_scroll_on_all_mobile_tables NO:hiding_all_but_first_column_without_expand; eg: 8-column_table_on_mobile >> card_per_row_with_key_fields

principle: Load dashboard widgets independently with per-widget skeleton states — a page-wide spinner blocks the entire dashboard while fast-loading widgets could already be useful
explanation: X.ui|dashboard_loading: page_spinner_for_all_widgets: all_blocked_until_slowest_loads; independent_loading: each_widget_fetches_independently -> fast_widgets_show_data_immediately; skeleton: per_widget_placeholder_matching_widget_layout; priority_loading: above_fold_widgets_load_first; stale_while_revalidate: show_cached_data_immediately+refresh_in_background; NO:wait_for_all_data_before_rendering NO:generic_full_page_spinner; eg: whole_dashboard_blocked_by_slow_analytics >> each_card_loads_and_shows_independently

principle: Use progressive disclosure in data tables: show summary row by default and expand for detail — most users need the summary 90% of the time and full detail only occasionally
explanation: X.ux|table_progressive_disclosure: full_detail_all_rows: overwhelming + slow_for_large_datasets; progressive: default_row: 3-4_key_columns+expand_chevron; expanded_row: full_detail_inline_below; bulk_actions: appear_when_rows_selected(sticky_action_bar_bottom); sorting: click_header+arrow_indicator; pagination_vs_infinite_scroll: pagination_for_task-oriented(support_tickets)+infinite_scroll_for_browsing(social_feed); NO:all_columns_visible_by_default NO:no_way_to_see_full_row_detail; eg: 12_column_table_all_visible >> 4_columns+expandable_row_detail

principle: Group dashboard widgets into information layers: summary at top, detail in middle, raw data at bottom — users scan top-to-bottom from overview to specifics, and raw data shown first creates cognitive overload
explanation: X.ui|dashboard_information_layers: raw_data_at_top: user_must_process_details_before_context; information_hierarchy: (1)summary_KPIs: key_numbers_at_a_glance (2)trend_charts: patterns_over_time (3)segment_breakdowns: which_group_is_driving_the_number (4)raw_tables: drill_down_for_individual_records; visual_grouping: each_layer_has_section_header+consistent_spacing; tabs_or_scroll: summary_always_visible+detail_behind_tab_or_scroll; NO:tables_at_top_of_dashboard NO:mixing_summary_and_detail_without_grouping; eg: raw_event_table_first >> KPI_summary+charts+segments+table
